name: galaxy-zoo-kids-desi

env_axis.name: instrument
env_axis.shift_mechanism: same galaxies imaged by two surveys (KiDS deeper/higher-res; DESI shallower/lower-res) on the GAMA fields

ood_protocol.name: kids-desi-cross-instrument
ood_protocol.ref: Walmsley et al. 2024 (Zoobot cross-instrument); Galaxy Zoo DESI papers

facts: Same galaxies imaged by KiDS (deeper, higher-res) and DESI (shallower, lower-res) on GAMA fields; Zoobot CNN baseline.
hypotheses: Morphology classification trained on KiDS does not fully transfer to DESI of the same galaxies, because the instrument gap produces a physical effect (different features visible at different depths) rather than a pure covariate shift.
Cross-instrument shift on the same galaxies acts as a label-stability test, so method behavior here bounds generalization to new galaxies only weakly.
intuitions: Zoobot extensions from 2024-25 and Galaxy Zoo DESI follow-ups likely exist.
LSST early-release morphology classifications that effectively benchmark KiDS/DESI models probably exist.
unknowns: Cross-instrument shift on the same galaxies decouples population shift from image-formation shift — perhaps the cleanest such pair in the corpus — but there is no established label for this 'label-stable observation shift'.
Citizen-science annotator disagreement is not separately exposed.
epistemic_state.boundary: Two-instrument only; cannot generalize to three-way (with LSST or Euclid) without additional data.

representational_commitments: Citizen-science labels aggregated as vote distribution
materialization_status: Galaxy Zoo 1 DR table5 CSV (3.6 MB) only — early historical release. Galaxy Zoo DESI + KiDS volunteer catalogs deferred (need either the data.galaxyzoo.org direct file list or Walmsley 2024 Zoobot inference outputs).
